channel_title: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
title: Your AI Agent Is Locked To One Model. OpenClaw Just Killed That.
executive_summary: In April 2026, OpenClaw, an open-source agent framework, matured significantly, allowing it to execute complex multi-step workflows and becoming a serious runtime environment. This development, led by Peter and his team, enables OpenClaw to move beyond being just a viral demo and towards handling real work. The model layer underneath OpenClaw is becoming increasingly contested, with Anthropic and OpenAI making strategic choices that impact how models are used and paid for.
bullets: OpenClaw's growth is compared to a teenager growing up, getting the keys to a car, and developing a prefrontal cortex to make responsible decisions, symbolizing its increased maturity and ability to handle complex tasks. This growth allows OpenClaw to move from being a simple architecture to a more sophisticated system capable of orchestrating complex workflows.

The concept of a 'durable workflow' is introduced, which refers to a workflow that can survive changes in models, subscription plans, and provider policies, and has its own identity with inputs, outputs, permissions, tools, state, review steps, and memory. This is crucial for serious work, as it allows the workflow to continue operating even if the underlying model changes.

Anthropic's April move to limit the use of Claude, their model, for always-on third-party agents at scale, is seen as a strategic choice to protect their margins and capacity. This move is unpopular with the developer community but is rational from Anthropic's perspective, as they want to ensure that Claude is used in a way that is sustainable for their business.

OpenAI, on the other hand, is taking a different approach, making Codex available as part of the Chat GPT subscription across all paid tiers, and allowing OpenClaw users to route work to Chat GPT and Codex. This move reinforces OpenAI's agent strategy and makes their infrastructure feel like a natural place for OpenClaw workflows to land.

Google's Gemma 4, launched under Apache 2.0, is positioned as an open model for advanced reasoning, agentic workflows, and on-device use. This provides builders with a credible local branch of the runtime tree to work with, and allows for the use of different models for different steps in a workflow.

The concept of 'memory' is critical in OpenClaw, as it needs to be user-owned and not locked to a specific model or provider. This is achieved through the use of an 'open brain' recipe, which defines how the agent retrieves and writes memory, and provides providence for the memory, making it operational and trustworthy.

The 'open brain' recipe for OpenClaw is now live in the open-source GitHub repo, and provides a way for users to build durable workflows that can survive model churn and pricing changes. This recipe includes code review memory, task flow worklog, and memory and providence recipes, which help to make the agent's memory architecture more robust and reliable.
key_takeaways: OpenClaw has matured into a serious runtime environment, capable of executing complex multi-step workflows.
The model layer underneath OpenClaw is contested, with Anthropic and OpenAI making strategic choices that impact how models are used and paid for.
Durable workflows are crucial for serious work, as they can survive changes in models, subscription plans, and provider policies.
Memory needs to be user-owned and not locked to a specific model or provider, and can be achieved through the use of an 'open brain' recipe.
Builders should focus on building vertical work loops on top of the runtime, rather than just making another shallow wrapper.
The scarce asset is not just access to a model, but ownership of the memory, tools, permissions, and operating rhythm around the model.

technical_terms.OpenClaw: An open-source agent framework that allows for the execution of complex workflows.
technical_terms.LLM: Large Language Model, a type of artificial intelligence model used for natural language processing.
technical_terms.Anthropic: A company that develops and provides AI models, including Claude.
technical_terms.OpenAI: A company that develops and provides AI models, including Chat GPT and Codex.
technical_terms.Gemma 4: An open model launched by Google for advanced reasoning, agentic workflows, and on-device use.
technical_terms.Codex: A model developed by OpenAI for coding and programming tasks.
technical_terms.Chat GPT: A conversational AI model developed by OpenAI.
